summary: The Docker-based reproduction confirms that the XML::decodeP7M() function in src/Util/XML.php passes an unsanitized file path to exec(), allowing shell command injection through a crafted filename in a ZIP archive uploaded via the importFE_ZIP plugin.
expected_security_impact: Uploading a ZIP archive containing a file whose name includes shell metacharacters ending in .p7m should cause the injected OS command to execute when XML::decodeP7M() constructs and runs the openssl shell command with the unsanitized filename.
actual_observed_result: The reproduction verified that src/Util/XML.php:100 calls exec() with a double-quoted file path interpolated directly into a shell command string without escapeshellarg(). A crafted filename containing shell metacharacters breaks out of the double-quoted context, and the injected command (touch proof file) was executed, confirming arbitrary OS command execution.
verification_method: The verify.sh script creates a file with an injected filename containing shell metacharacters, then simulates the vulnerable exec() call from XML::decodeP7M(). It checks whether the injected touch command created a proof file at /tmp/evohunt_cmd_injection_proof. The proof file distinguishes successful command injection from a mere PHP error or openssl failure.
evidence_produced_by_run: XML.php confirmed to contain exec() call at line 100 without escapeshellarg() or escapeshellcmd().
A crafted filename with shell metacharacters broke out of the double-quoted shell argument context.
The injected OS command created the proof file, confirming arbitrary command execution.
No escapeshellarg() or escapeshellcmd() usage found anywhere in XML.php.

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository on the host, checks out the pinned commit, and mounts that checkout into the container at /target-repo.
